prediction.reasoning: The price prediction and confidence intervals are calculated using the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. Historical daily price data for AMT over the past two years was analyzed to estimate the annualized drift (mean daily log return) and volatility (standard deviation of daily log returns). With a current price of $190.05 and a prediction horizon of 18 trading days, the GBM model projects the stock price to follow a log-normal distribution. The point estimate provided is the median of this projected distribution. The confidence intervals represent the ranges within which the price is expected to fall with 50%, 80%, and 90% probability, respectively, based on the estimated historical parameters. This model assumes that historical price patterns (drift and volatility) will continue into the future, which may not always be the case due to market changes or unforeseen events.
